meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop spinning your wheels in tutorial hell. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your SaaS MVP with expert project management workflows.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By treating your MVP as a managed project rather than a coding exercise, you force technical clarity. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution to bridge the gap between learning concepts and generating recurring monthly revenue.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' tutorial, and for the tenth time this month, you feel like you're building, yet you’ve shipped nothing. It’s the silent killer of SaaS ventures: tutorial hell. You’re accumulating syntax knowledge while your market window closes and your competitors gain ground. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder isn't knowing how to write a function; it's knowing how to architect a solution that solves a real-world problem.

To break this cycle, you must shift your mindset from 'learning' to 'managing.' We aren't just coding; we are engineering a business asset. By integrating Antigravity for high-level architectural planning and Claude Code for rapid-fire execution, you reclaim your autonomy. This is how you move from a collection of fragmented snippets to a deployed, revenue-ready product.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, document the core feature that solves a burning problem. Use Antigravity to map the user flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a lean MVP scope for a SaaS product that solves [Insert Problem]. Create a high-level architecture diagram and a feature priority list focusing on revenue-generating actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP scope and core database schema based on the Antigravity architecture.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Technical Infrastructure
steps.description: Don't build from scratch. Use Antigravity to generate the foundational boilerplate that ensures scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a Next.js 14+ app with Supabase authentication, Tailwind CSS, and Stripe integration. Ensure type safety throughout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project directory and install dependencies based on the Antigravity boilerplate structure.'

steps.title: Execute Feature Modules Iteratively
steps.description: Break the project into 4-hour sprints. Use Claude Code to build one module at a time, ensuring it is fully tested.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the User Authentication module into 4-hour engineering tasks. Provide specific acceptance criteria for each task.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the user sign-up flow with Supabase integration and add unit tests for edge cases.'

steps.title: Implement Feedback Loops
steps.description: Validate your progress by simulating user interactions. If a feature doesn't directly contribute to the user goal, cut it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase and identify any 'feature creep' that is not essential for the MVP. Suggest removals to streamline the build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the dashboard component to simplify the UI based on the feedback loops defined in the architectural review.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and Monitoring
steps.description: Configure automated CI/CD pipelines to ensure you can ship updates daily without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel. Include error monitoring integration instructions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Set up the .github/workflows/main.yml file and ensure all build scripts are correctly referenced for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Treat every code block as a liability; if it doesn't solve a customer problem, delete it immediately.
Use Claude Code to write tests before the implementation logic; this prevents the 'tutorial trap' of writing code that works but isn't robust.
Always maintain a 'Decision Log' in your repository to track why you chose specific libraries; it saves weeks of debugging later.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over 'Time-to-Code-Completion'; focus on the checkout flow before the dashboard UI.
Leverage Antigravity to simulate 'what-if' scenarios in your architecture before you commit to a database schema.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a feedback loop with 5 beta testers to validate your revenue model before adding new features.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap. It is time to turn that idea into a recurring revenue engine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
